title: The Crew Enters the Climbing Current
description: On pages 4–6, the crew enters the climbing current as the crew reaches Reverse Mountain and is dragged into the impossible currents that lead to the Grand Line.
scene: The Crew Enters the Climbing Current. The crew reaches Reverse Mountain and is dragged into the impossible currents that lead to the Grand Line. The scene focuses on the immediate character conflict rather than background exposition.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: In the first place, why do we even have to enter directly through the entrance anyways?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Couldn't we just head directly South and still enter?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: No we can't!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: That's right, and there's a perfectly good reason for that...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: It's so much cooler to go in using the real entrance!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Are you an idiot!?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: That's not the reason!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Wait, what? Guys! The storm suddenly stopped!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Hey you're right.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: What?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: That can't be...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: that storm should have directly led us to the entrance...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Wow, such a nice day!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: I wonder what happened to the storm?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Haha haha!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Oh no!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: We've entered calm belt...!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Calm Belt?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: What's that?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Hey, look, even though the storm's still going on over there,

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: It's perfectly calm over here...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: This is no time to be just standing about! Go grab the oars and start rowing as fast as you can!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: We have to return back to the storm immediately!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: As you wish, NAM!! W

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: What're you getting so worked up about? And you know this is a sail ship, not a rowboat, right?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Why would we even want to go back into the storm?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Just shut up and do as I say!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: But the weather's so nice here...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Fine, then I'll give you your explanation! Right now, we've drifted south exactly as you suggested doing!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Oh, so we're in a Grand Line now?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: If it were that easy, anybody could just come to Grand Line whenever they wanted!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Listen! The Grand Line is surrounded by two ocean belts.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Calm belt

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Calm belt

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: And those two windless belts known as the calm belt are exactly where we're stuck in right now!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Calm, eh? Sure seems that way with no wind and all

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: So? What's your point?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: The point is that...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Ahh! Is this an earth- quake?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: But we're out in the open seas!

summary: This part advances the chapter by focusing on the crew enters the climbing current. It keeps the main tension clear while preserving the dialogue that drives the scene.
